stimulus: Author Octavia Butler, ______blank to establish a sense of disorientation that unsettles the reader, opens her novel Kindred with a stark account of "the day I lost my arm" and the disquieting return of an unfamiliar room.
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
rationale:
Choice C is the best answer. The convention being tested is the use of verb forms within a sentence. The nonfinite present participle verb "working" is correctly used to form a subordinate clause that describes the intent behind how Butler begins her novel.

Choice A is incorrect because the finite past-tense verb "worked" can’t be used in this way to form a subordinate clause. Choice B is incorrect because the finite present-tense verb "works" can’t be used in this way to form a subordinate clause. Choice D is incorrect because the finite present-progressive verb "is working" can’t be used in this way to form a subordinate clause.
